title: cynoglossum australe var. australe
intro: Cynoglossum australe var. australe, also known as the Australian Forget-me-not or Forked Forget-me-not, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its blue flowers with a distinctive forked lower petal. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Cynoglossum australe var. australe is typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution should be exercised as excessive consumption may cause adverse effects. In moderation, the plant's herbal preparations may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as support immune function.
